data.executiveSummary: The frontend-web service experienced significant latency issues due to an N+1 query problem involving repeated database queries.

rootCause.title: N+1 Query Issue
rootCause.description: The distributed tracing data reveals an N+1 query problem causing excessive sequential database calls to the 'users_cart' table during the execution of GET /api/checkout requests.

evidenceChain: Multiple sequential SELECT * FROM users_cart queries were executed in each trace.
High and sustained p95 latency of 4500ms for the affected requests.

timeline.event: Incident started with traces showing high latency.
Continued high latency confirmed by repetitive database query patterns.

data.impact: Users experienced delayed responses during checkout operations, leading to potential disruptions in user service and satisfaction.
recommendedActions: Implement eager loading for related data in 'users_cart' to resolve the N+1 query issue and reduce latency.
Monitor CPU usage and optimize resource allocation to prevent future performance bottlenecks.
